fields.doc: Frame format version. Single byte. Currently 1. Decoder MUST throw FrameVersionMismatchException on any other value. Bumping the version is a wire-breaking change that requires every consumer to handle both versions during the rollout window.
Byte count of the UTF-8 encoded kid that follows. Single byte (max 255). Decoder validates against PayloadCryptoKeyring.MIN_KID_LENGTH / MAX_KID_LENGTH.
UTF-8 bytes of the key id used to encrypt this frame. Length is variable, declared by KID_LENGTH. The decoder uses kid to look up the AES-GCM key in the per-domain keyring.
AES-GCM nonce. Fixed 12 bytes (GCM-spec). Offset is variable — comes immediately after KID. Generated fresh per encrypt; no nonce reuse with the same key (GCM safety requirement).

Ciphertext followed by the AES-GCM authentication tag. Length is variable (frame_size - VERSION - KID_LENGTH - KID - NONCE). The trailing CONSTRAINT_TAG_LENGTH bytes are the authentication tag; the decoder slices them off before decrypting the rest.
